steps.title: Identify which terms are like terms
steps.content:
Drop the parentheses first:
$$(8nr^2+2nr)+(4n^2r+3nr)=8nr^2+2nr+4n^2r+3nr$$
Only $2nr$ and $3nr$ have the exact same variable part. The terms $8nr^2$ and $4n^2r$ are not like terms.

steps.title: Check with a Desmos table
steps.content:
Use a quick table check at two $(n,r)$ values:
$$n_1=[1,2]$$
$$r_1=[2,3]$$
$$o_1=(8n_1r_1^2+2n_1r_1)+(4n_1^2r_1+3n_1r_1)$$
$$d_1=8n_1r_1^2+4n_1^2r_1+5n_1r_1$$
The original and choice D give the same output list.

steps.title: Answer
steps.content: $8nr^2+4n^2r+5nr$ matches choice D, so D is correct.
